data.title: LyondellBasell Slashes Dividend Amid 'One Of The Longest Downturns In Chemical Industry'
data.description:
LyondellBasell Industries (NYSE:LYB) shares are down during Friday’s premarket session following the company’s announcement of a significant dividend reduction.

LYB Dividend Cut to 69 Cents Per Share

LyondellBasell’s board declared a quarterly dividend of 69 cents per share, a substantial decrease from the previous quarter’s dividend of $1.37 per share.

The dividend is payable on March 9, 2026, to shareholders of record date as of March 2, 2026.

This decision reflects the company’s strategy to recalibrate its financial commitments in light of ongoing market challenges.

The company aims to return 70% of its free cash flow to shareholders through the cycle, despite the current downturn in the industry.

During its fourth-quarter results release, the company said petrochemical markets faced headwinds in 2025 from global trade disruptions, falling oil prices, and capacity additions, which outpaced global demand growth.

Mixed Q4 Results

Entering the first quarter, the company said it is managing continued volatility in feedstock and energy prices and is aligning operating rates with global demand.

LYB Shows Short-Term Strength Above SMAs

The broader market experienced slight declines on the previous trading day, with the Dow Jones falling 0.21% and the Nasdaq down 0.17%. LyondellBasell’s stock is underperforming relative to the Industrials sector, which remained unchanged, suggesting that company-specific factors are influencing its price action.

The stock is currently trading 1.4% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 15% above its 100-day SMA, indicating some short-term strength. Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 28.71%, and they are currently positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, reflecting a challenging market environment.

The RSI is at 57.24, which is considered neutral territory, indicating that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, MACD shows a value of 2.2415, below its signal line at 2.3526, suggesting bearish pressure on the stock.

The combination of a neutral RSI and bearish MACD indicates mixed momentum, suggesting that traders should remain cautious.

  • Key Resistance: $61.50
  • Key Support: $47.50

LYB Financial Update and Analyst Outlook

LyondellBasell Industries is slated to provide its next financial update on April 24, 2026.

  • EPS Estimate: 25 cents (Down from 33 cents)
  • Revenue Estimate: $7.28 billion (Down from $7.68 billion)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Hold Rating with an average price target of $55.14. Recent analyst moves include:

  • RBC Capital: Sector Perform (Raises Target to $51.00) (Feb. 3)
  • Citigroup: Neutral (Raises Target to $49.00) (Feb. 3)
  • Wells Fargo: Equal-Weight (Raises Target to $48.00) (Feb. 2)

LYB Price Action: LyondellBasell Industries shares were down 1.48% at $54.51 during premarket trading on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Copart Slides After Q2 Miss, Shares Near 52-Week Lows
data.description:
• Copart shares are approaching critical lows. What’s behind CPRT weakness?

Earnings And Revenue Fall Short; Gross Profit And Net Income Slide

Copart reported earnings per share of 36 cents, missing the consensus estimate of 40 cents. In addition, the company reported revenue of $1.12 billion, missing the consensus estimate of $1.14 billion.

Revenue declined 3.6% year-over-year. Gross profit fell 6.2% to $492.8 million, while net income attributable to Copart decreased 9.5% to $350.7 million.

Technical Weakness Deepens As Shares Hover Near 52-Week Lows

Copart Inc is currently trading 14.2% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 17% below its 100-day SMA, indicating short-term bearishness amidst a longer-term downtrend. Over the past 12 months, the stock has declined by 35.33%, reflecting sustained selling pressure. Additionally, it is trading near its 52-week low of $36.52, suggesting that the stock is testing critical support levels.

The RSI stands at 38.58, indicating that the stock is neither oversold nor overbought, suggesting a lack of strong buying or selling momentum at this level. Meanwhile, the MACD is currently at -0.6242, below its signal line at -0.3064, which points to increasing bearish momentum and may indicate further declines if the stock does not stabilize.

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Copart, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: Moderate (Score: 55.29/100) — Reflects a fair valuation relative to market peers.
  • Quality: Strong (Score: 69.47/100) — Indicates a robust business model and solid financial health.
  • Momentum: Weak (Score: 7.73/100) — Stock is underperforming the broader market significantly.

The Verdict: Copart’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a mixed performance landscape. While the Quality score indicates a strong underlying business, the extremely low Momentum score suggests significant challenges ahead, potentially impacting short-term stock performance.

Copart Shares Edge Lower

CPRT Price Action: At the time of publication, Copart shares are trading 9.99% lower at $33.89, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: James Cameron Warns Netflix–WBD Deal Would Be A 'Sinking Ship' Moment For Theatrical Film Business
data.description:
“Titanic” and “Avatar” director James Cameron has reportedly expressed his apprehensions about Netflix‘s (NASDAQ:NFLX) proposed purchase of Warner Bros. Discovery‘s (NASDAQ:WBD) film studio in a letter.

In his letter, Cameron cautioned that a sale of Warner Bros. Discovery to Netflix would severely damage the theatrical film business, to which he has devoted his career. While acknowledging his films' success in streaming markets, he likened the potential fallout for cinemas to a "sinking ship."

Sen. Lee, who leads the Senate antitrust subcommittee, held a February 3 hearing on the proposed Netflix–Warner Bros. deal, after which Cameron sent a letter; Netflix co-CEO Ted Sarandos and a WBD executive testified at the session.

In its testimony, Netflix highlighted its planned $20 billion global film and TV spending for 2026, mostly in the U.S., and said a potential deal would boost, not cut, production investment, backed by a stronger combined company and expanded studio facilities.

All Eyes On Netflix

On Wednesday, a report said that the Department of Justice (DOJ) is currently probing the impact of Warner Bros. Discovery’s potential sale on theatre businesses and has summoned some of the largest theatre chain owners in the country to assess the outcome.

Disclaimer: This content was partially produced with the help of AI tools and was reviewed and published by Benzinga editors.

Image via Shutterstock
